hypothesis: O1-ADRACT — Active-address / tx-count network-activity momentum (lagged network-growth momentum timer)
backlog_ref: D5-16 sibling (active-address); tested here as a TIMER, not a Metcalfe valuation ratio

data.catalog_check: community-api.coinmetrics.io/v4/catalog-v2/asset-metrics — AdrActCnt & TxCnt both community:true, 1d, BTC 2009+, ETH 2015+
data.causality: feature LAGGED >=1 day; NEXT-day forward return; expanding (no-lookahead) orthogonalization

honest_N_note: 297 raw-momentum configs + 297 price-momentum-orthogonalized configs, searched per asset
key_diagnostic: Raw network-activity momentum is 0.55-0.73 correlated with PRICE momentum (BTC) and 0.26-0.70 (ETH). Orthogonalizing to price momentum collapses the forward-return IC from ~0.04-0.06 to ~0.01-0.02. This is the documented reverse-causality echo: on-chain activity lags price, so 'network momentum' is largely repackaged price momentum.

orth_btc.vs_BH: 0.963 vs 0.953 (does NOT beat B&H or random-lottery 0.996)
orth_btc.verdict: KILL — adoption-only signal cannot beat buy&hold

binding_gate: baselines (BTC adoption-only cannot beat buy&hold) / holdout (ETH negative OOS)
rationale: No honest pre-registered (N=1) configuration survives: all four canonical variants have null surrogate p and/or negative consume-once holdout. The only PROMISING flag (raw BTC IS-best) is a multiple-testing selection artifact (PBO=0.50, DSR p=0.80). The clean adoption-leads-price test (orthogonalized to price momentum) KILLs on both assets — BTC can't beat B&H, ETH's OOS Sharpe is -0.19. The edge is the price-momentum echo (reverse causality), not network adoption.
